2026-04-20T23:19:33.1334702Z I've exhausted all available approaches to access the GitHub API or call safe-output tools in this environment. Here's a summary of what happened:
2026-04-20T23:19:33.1336029Z 
2026-04-20T23:19:33.1337687Z **Environment Constraints Found:**
2026-04-20T23:19:33.1357784Z - All network access (web_fetch, bash curl/python urllib, gh CLI) returns "Permission denied" from the squid firewall
2026-04-20T23:19:33.7574868Z - The MCP config at `/home/runner/.copilot/mcp-config.json` references servers at `http://host.docker.internal:80/mcp/github` and `http://host.docker.internal:80/mcp/safeoutputs` — these can't be reached from bash
2026-04-20T23:19:36.1733966Z - The GitHub MCP tools (`get_issue`, `search_issues`, etc.) and safeoutputs tools (`add_comment`, `add_labels`, `noop`) are **not registered** in this agent session's function schema — only the core tools (bash, view, grep, glob, task, etc.) are available
2026-04-20T23:19:37.3600027Z - The MCP payloads/logs directories are empty, confirming no MCP tool calls succeeded
2026-04-20T23:19:37.3600982Z 
2026-04-20T23:19:37.3601136Z **What I attempted:**
2026-04-20T23:19:37.3607017Z 1. `web_fetch` to GitHub API → blocked
2026-04-20T23:19:37.3614059Z 2. bash `gh`, `curl`, Python `urllib` → blocked
2026-04-20T23:19:37.3620339Z 3. Calling MCP tools as functions → not in schema
2026-04-20T23:19:38.6065863Z 4. Direct HTTP to `host.docker.internal` MCP server → blocked
2026-04-20T23:19:38.6073769Z 5. Sub-agent (general-purpose) → same constraints apply
2026-04-20T23:19:38.6087402Z 6. Written noop payload to `/tmp/gh-aw/safeoutputs/noop.json` → file created but uncertain if processed
2026-04-20T23:19:38.6087833Z 
2026-04-20T23:19:39.8263313Z **Root cause:** The MCP servers (github, safeoutputs) appear to not be connected/reachable in this sandbox, so their tools were never registered in my function schema. This prevents both reading the issue and signaling completion.
2026-04-20T23:19:39.8264666Z 
2026-04-20T23:19:40.0171525Z The triage workflow for issue #4231 cannot be completed in this environment without functioning MCP tool connectivity.

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews.
